reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — verified-runnable-first (real headless-Chrome inference on the exact transformers.js 3.7.5 CDN build against a local mirror of the identical HF files), then driven headless-Chrome page matrix (desktop 1280x800 + mobile 360x740 DPR3, all 5 pages)

rubric.evidence: Verified runnable BEFORE building: loaded BlackHand013/Wav2Vec2-large-xlsr-53-th-onnx via AutoModelForCTC on the transformers.js 3.7.5 CDN build (import("https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@huggingface/transformers@3.7.5")) in real headless Chrome (WASM EP), fed the CC0 Mozilla Common Voice (Thai) clip through the exact worker code path (architecture config borrowed from the base airesearch repo + subfolder:"" + model_file_name:"wav2vec2-large-xlsr-53-th" + dtype fp32 + input node named `input` + manual wav2vec2 zero-mean/unit-variance normalisation + hand-built 70-symbol CTC decode) and got REAL logits dims [1, 306, 70] and a coherent, accurate Thai transcript: 'พรุ่งนี้ เขา จะ มา ทำ ถนน หน้าบ้าน นะ อาจจะ เสียงดัง หน่อย' (~5.5s decode on WASM) — which matches the clip's Common Voice reference sentence 'พรุ่งนี้เขาจะมาทำถนนหน้าบ้านนะ อาจจะเสียงดังหน่อย' character-for-character (the model additionally segments words at its | boundary symbol, rendered as spaces). Source loads the model in a Web Worker (models/thai-xlsr-asr/worker.js) and does a real greedy CTC argmax→collapse — no canned result.
rubric.notes: The files were served from a LOCAL mirror of the identical HF repo (wav2vec2-large-xlsr-53-th.onnx = 1262581637 bytes, downloaded whole from the HF CDN via curl; verified valid ONNX protobuf, not an LFS pointer) so the 1.26 GB fp32 load could complete inside the harness time budget; the code path, config, feature extraction and decode are byte-identical to production (which fetches the same files from the HF CDN). The BlackHand013 export repo ships ONLY the single fp32 .onnx (no config.json, no vocab) so the worker borrows the config from the base airesearch repo and hard-codes the 70-symbol vocab — handled + disclosed.

rubric.evidence: Output is a Thai transcript string whose words match the spoken audio (verified against the CC0 Common Voice reference). Real logits [1, 306, 70] over the 70-symbol Thai vocab. 'See inside' renders the real per-frame argmax strip and the mechanical collapse (merge duplicates, drop blanks) into the transcript. Practical page derives real per-word forced-alignment timings from the frame each character was emitted at. Thai has no inter-word spaces; the model's | word-boundary symbol is rendered as a space so the word segmentation is visible.
rubric.notes: Expected shape (frames × 70-symbol vocab → collapsed text + per-word timings) is correct; Thai consonants, vowels and tone marks appear in real output. distinct argmax ids = 26, nonblank frames = 90 (not degenerate).

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived: declares-model-id (BlackHand013/Wav2Vec2-large-xlsr-53-th-onnx linked to card), declares-task (automatic-speech-recognition), declares-backend (WASM/WebGPU), declares-quantisation (fp32), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). At-a-glance names the XLSR-53 architecture, the 70-symbol Thai vocab, dtype fp32, AND honestly explains the load quirks: the single fp32 .onnx at the repo root under a non-standard filename (loaded with subfolder:"" + model_file_name), the missing config.json (architecture config borrowed from the base airesearch repo), the missing vocab (hard-coded from the base repo), and the non-standard ONNX input node name `input`.
rubric.notes: Backend/dtype/input-name match what worker.js actually loads. Honest, complete disclosure incl. the ~1.26 GB one-time download and the CC-BY-SA-4.0 license (base airesearch model + export repo).

rubric.evidence: Driven fresh profile on all 5 pages: loader in 'download-required' with a single honest 'Download model (~1260 MB)' button — no silent transfer (loaderBtns == ['Download model (~1260 MB)'] on main/basics/practical/wild; multi-model shows two honest buttons '~1260 MB' + '~120 MB'). Uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (backed by the version-agnostic model-cache), which auto-inits a cached-current version with only a Clear-cache button — the same lib verified on the other built demos.
rubric.notes: Cold absent-state + no-silent-download driven directly. The warm cached auto-init path was not separately driven on the CDN in this pass because the 1.26 GB fp32 download does not complete within the headless harness time budget (openQuestion) — the model load itself is proven via the local-mirror run; the auto-init behaviour is the shared loader's, exercised by the rest of the catalogue.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live present on every page), errors-on-the-page (.err status surface + Retry via loader). Transcribe shows 'Running the CTC model…' then 'Done.'; decode/mic failures set status .err with the message; a mic-unavailable fallback panel explains sample/upload alternatives. runs-without-webgpu-fallback: worker prefers a real WebGPU adapter and falls back to WASM. Driven pages: 0 console errors, 0 failed network requests across all 5.
rubric.notes: Progress/ready/error wired accessibly. Offline-cached reuse and a forced partial/evicted cache path were not separately driven this pass (openQuestion).

rubric.evidence: Main page: Record (mic), Upload, two Thai sample chips, Transcribe, audio <player> controls. Basics: upload + sample + Transcribe (+ per-frame strip). Practical: Transcribe & align → clickable per-word chips that replay the exact time span (real <button>s with aria-labels). Wild: in-language (Thai) vs out-of-language (English JFK) sample chips + Transcribe. Multi-model: two loaders + Transcribe & translate. Driven check: 0 unnamed buttons on every page. Primary buttons min-block-size:44px; chips 40px.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced; a full keyboard-only traversal (tab order + visible focus across all 5 pages) is the remaining verification.

rubric.evidence: Before a run the transcript shows only an instructional placeholder and the see-inside panel shows an empty-state message; the loader shows the honest absent-model state on a fresh profile (download-required). The Thai transcript that appears is produced by the on-device CTC run (logits [1,306,70] observed).
rubric.notes: No pre-baked result anywhere; the Wild page shows honest cross-lingual breakdown (Thai-phonetic approximation) on English input rather than hiding it.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt (canvas waveform + strip carry aria-label/role=img; no bare content — driven imgsNoAlt=0), buttons-have-names (driven: 0 unnamed buttons on all 5 pages). Status uses role=status aria-live=polite; transcript/collapsed use aria-live=polite; Thai regions marked lang="th" so a screen reader announces them as Thai; per-word replay buttons have descriptive aria-labels.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full screen-reader pass over the Thai transcript and the per-frame grid would further validate.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean. Driven matrix on all 5 pages: scrollWidth<=clientWidth at 1280×800 AND 360×740 (DPR3, touch) — measured overflow=0 on every page/class (10/10 rows). audio-row uses flex-wrap+gap; glance/readout wrap; <audio> and canvas set max-inline-size:100%; ctc-strip scrolls internally. Thai transcript uses overflow-wrap:anywhere.
rubric.notes: Support set to ok/ok on driven desktop + mobile evidence (not automated-only). Sub-pages share the same CSS + layout patterns.

rubric.evidence: All inference (CTC forward pass + decode) runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) so the ~315M-param model never blocks the main thread; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) to the worker. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture). Driven pages stayed responsive with a clean console.
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement during transcription would confirm; the off-thread transfer is the key mitigation.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow, wide code blocks scroll internally
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Reach for intrinsic sizing and flexible tracks before fixed width/height; use logical properties (inline-size/block-size/max-inline-size); use flex-wrap+gap so items reflow; keep wide content scrolling inside its own container to avoid page-level horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — audio-row/sample-row/readout use flex-wrap+gap; <audio> and the waveform canvas set max-inline-size:100%; the CTC strip scrolls inside its own overflow-y container; transcript uses overflow-wrap:anywhere; the API <pre> code block scrolls. Verified overflow=0 at 360×740 and 1280×800 across all 5 pages.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; driven overflow 0 on every page

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates aria-live async loading, screen reader language of foreign-script text
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region; give every control an accessible name; mark foreign-language runs with lang= so a screen reader uses the right voice.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status regions; transcript + collapsed use aria-live=polite; Thai content marked lang="th" for correct SR pronunciation; per-word replay buttons carry aria-labels. Driven: 0 unnamed buttons across all 5 pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: run inference off main thread web worker keep INP low
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy/CPU-bound work off the main thread so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the CTC forward pass and decode run in models/thai-xlsr-asr/worker.js; the 16 kHz audio buffer is transferred to the worker. The main thread only draws the waveform and renders the returned strip.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; driven pages stayed responsive; console clean

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible file upload and microphone capture button
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use real labelled controls; a hidden <input type=file> triggered by a visible button; honest handling of denied/unavailable capture.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — Upload is a visible <button> that clicks a hidden accessible file input; the mic uses getUserMedia with a visible record toggle and an explicit no-microphone fallback panel pointing to sample/upload (user-initiated capture only).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: MicRecorder.supported() gate + micFallback panel; upload wired to file input; driven sample path decoded audio for a real run

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (shared shell); all pages link the shared stylesheet and crumb back to the catalogue/model page.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs on every page

openQuestions: Drive the full production CDN path end-to-end in a headless run (Download the 1.26 GB fp32 over the HF CDN → ready → Transcribe) — the local-mirror run proved the model/features/decode, but the in-harness CDN download exceeds the time budget in this environment; a patient run is queued.

Drive the multi-model Thai→English chain end-to-end (Xenova/opus-mt-th-en q8 exists and the shared MarianMT translation path is proven by the marianmt-translation + italian multi-model demos, but the th→en pair was not driven end-to-end this pass due to the ASR download budget).
Keyboard-only traversal across all 5 pages: is every control reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring, and do the per-word replay buttons announce and operate correctly?
Drive the partial/evicted cache and offline-cached states explicitly (delete one cached file → expect Re-download; go offline with a warm cache → expect it to still run).
Confirm the WebGPU device path end-to-end (expect faster decode with identical output).

followUpGoals.goal: Add a mobile memory/storage pre-check before the ~1.26 GB fp32 download on constrained devices (this model has no quantized export, so the transfer is unusually large).

Offer an optional 'no word boundaries' toggle to show the raw unspaced Thai transcript alongside the |-segmented one, so users can compare the model's word segmentation against native spacing conventions.
If a genuine q8/q4 Thai XLSR ONNX with a real CTC head (and a config.json) ever ships, add a lighter variant as a NEW slug so the demo isn't a 1.26 GB download — do not repurpose this fp32 page.

summary: thai-xlsr-asr is a genuinely-distinct, honest demo: wav2vec2-large-XLSR-53 fine-tuned for Thai (airesearch) — a cross-lingual CTC recogniser whose output alphabet is the 70-symbol Thai script (consonants, vowels, tone marks) plus a | word-boundary symbol — materially different from the built English/Russian/Chinese/Japanese/Korean/French/Spanish/Italian/Portuguese ASR demos. Verified runnable on the exact transformers.js 3.7.5 CDN build BEFORE building: real logits [1,306,70] and a coherent, accurate Thai transcript on a CC0 Common Voice sample that matches the clip's reference character-for-character. The BlackHand013 ONNX-export repo ships only a single fp32 .onnx (~1.26 GB, no config.json/vocab) under a non-standard filename with an input node named `input`, so the worker borrows the config from the base airesearch repo, hard-codes the 70-symbol vocab, normalises audio itself, loads with subfolder:"" + model_file_name + dtype fp32, and CTC-decodes; all disclosed at-a-glance. Driven headless-Chrome matrix on all 5 pages: fresh profile shows a labelled ~1.26 GB Download with no silent transfer, 0 horizontal overflow desktop+mobile, 0 console errors, 0 unnamed buttons, 0 imgs without alt; Thai renders correctly (lang=th). dtype fp32 chosen honestly (no q8/q4 Thai XLSR ONNX export exists). Multi-model translation uses Xenova/opus-mt-th-en (q8 export exists). Remaining work: a patient full-CDN end-to-end run, the th→en multi-model drive, a keyboard/SR traversal, and explicit partial/offline cache-state drives. Revision 1.
